answer: Anthropic is in the middle of one of the most consequential stretches in its history. The company filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC on June 1, 2026, targeting a Q4 2026 IPO at a valuation near $965 billion — making it the most valuable AI startup in the world, ahead of OpenAI. Revenue has grown explosively, with an annualized run rate hitting $47 billion by May 2026, up from roughly $10 billion just twelve months earlier. On the product front, Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 on June 9 — making its previously withheld Mythos-class model available to enterprise customers and paid subscribers at significantly reduced pricing ($10/M input, $50/M output tokens, less than half the prior cost). The company has also been unusually candid about AI safety risks: it published a report warning that AI models could soon autonomously design more capable successors, and called for a coordinated global mechanism to pause self-improving AI. Operationally, Anthropic now reports that over 80% of its own codebase is written by Claude — a striking demonstration of recursive AI development. Its Project Glasswing enterprise security initiative has expanded to 150+ organizations, and the company formalized a new partner ecosystem with a Services Track and Partner Hub to scale its enterprise go-to-market.
